text: Lesson 34
zh: 第34课

text: A happy discovery
zh: 一次令人惊喜的发现

text: What was the 'happy discovery' ?
zh: 这个'令人惊喜的发现'是什么？

text: Antique shops exert a peculiar fascination on a great many people.
zh: 古董店对许多人都有一种特殊的吸引力。

text: The more expensive kind of antique shop where rare objects are beautifully displayed in glass cases
zh: 那些较为高档的古董店，把珍稀物品精美地陈列在玻璃柜中，

text: to keep them free from dust is usually a forbidding place.
zh: 以防落上灰尘，通常给人一种拒人千里之外的感觉。

text: But no one has to muster up courage to enter a less pretentious antique shop.
zh: 但要走进一家不那么讲究的古董店，谁都无需鼓起什么勇气。

text: There is always hope that in its labyrinth of musty, dark, disordered rooms a real rarity will be found amongst the piles of assorted junk that litter the floors.
zh: 人们总抱着希望，在那迷宫般昏暗、霉味弥漫、杂乱无章的房间里，能在满地各式各样的破烂杂物中找到真正的珍品。

text: No one discovers a rarity by chance.
zh: 没有人是靠运气发现珍品的。

text: A truly dedicated bargain hunter must have patience,
zh: 一个真正执着的淘宝者必须要有耐心，

text: and above all, the ability to recognize the worth of something when he sees it.
zh: 更重要的是，要有一眼识货的眼力。

text: To do this, he must be at least as knowledgeable as the dealer.
zh: 要做到这一点，他至少要和店主一样内行。

text: Like a scientist bent on making a discovery,
zh: 他必须像一位一心想有所发现的科学家那样，

text: he must cherish the hope that one day he will be amply rewarded.
zh: 满怀希望，相信总有一天会得到丰厚的回报。

text: My old friend, Frank Halliday, is just such a person.
zh: 我的老朋友弗兰克·哈利迪就是这样一个人。

text: He has often described to me how he picked up a masterpiece for a mere ￡50.
zh: 他常跟我讲起，他是如何仅花50英镑就淘到一幅名画的。

text: One Saturday morning, Frank visited an antique shop in my neighbourhood.
zh: 一个星期六的上午，弗兰克到我家附近的一家古董店去。

text: As he had never been there before, he found a great deal to interest him.
zh: 由于他以前从未去过那儿，发现了许多引起他兴趣的东西。

text: The morning passed rapidly and Frank was about to leave when he noticed a large packing case lying on the floor.
zh: 上午的时光飞快地过去了，弗兰克正准备离开时，注意到地上放着一只大货箱。

text: The dealer told him that it had just come in, but that he could not be bothered to open it.
zh: 店主告诉他，这箱货刚到，他还没顾上打开。

text: Frank begged him to do so and the dealer reluctantly prised it open.
zh: 弗兰克恳求他打开看看，店主便勉强把箱子撬开了。

text: The contents were disappointing.
zh: 箱子里的东西令人失望。

text: Apart from an interesting-looking carved dagger, the box was full of crockery, much of it broken.
zh: 除了一把样式别致的雕花匕首外，箱子里装满了陶器，大多还是破损的。

text: Frank gently lifted the crockery out of the box and suddenly noticed a miniature painting at the bottom of the packing case.
zh: 弗兰克小心翼翼地把陶器一件件搬出箱子，突然发现箱底有一幅微型画。

text: As its composition and line reminded him of an Italian painting he knew well, he decided to buy it.
zh: 这幅画的构图和线条使他想起了自己熟悉的一幅意大利名画，于是他决定把它买下来。

text: Glancing at it briefly the dealer told him that it was worth ￡50.
zh: 店主只是瞟了一眼，就告诉他这幅画值50英镑。

text: Frank could hardly conceal his excitement, for he knew that he had made a real discovery.
zh: 弗兰克几乎无法掩饰自己的兴奋，因为他知道自己确实有了一个重大发现。

text: The tiny painting proved to be an unknown masterpiece by Correggio and was worth hundreds and thousands of pounds.
zh: 后来证实，这幅小画正是柯勒乔一幅不为人知的杰作，价值成千上万英镑。
